note: Hand-curated executive/world-leader entries — not legislators, but unavoidable on any AI-policy tracker.

politicians.name: Donald Trump
politicians.summary: Trump rescinded Biden's 2023 AI safety executive order on day one and replaced it with the Stargate-aligned Removing Barriers to American Leadership in AI EO, which directs agencies to clear regulations slowing AI development. His administration has tied federal procurement and export-control posture to American AI dominance, while pushing to expand domestic data-centre and chip-fab capacity.
keyPoints: Executive Order 14179 (Jan. 2025): Removed Biden-era AI Bill of Rights guardrails and ordered agencies to identify rules that hinder AI development.
Endorsed the Stargate Project — a $500B private commitment to US AI data-centre buildout led by OpenAI, SoftBank, and Oracle.
Designated AI as a national-security priority in revised export-control posture toward China and PRC-aligned chip suppliers.
Backed expansion of federal lands for data-centre siting and accelerated NEPA review for AI infrastructure projects.

researchedBills.title: Removing Barriers to American Leadership in Artificial Intelligence
researchedBills.summary: Day-one executive order rescinding Biden's 2023 AI safety EO and directing agencies to identify and remove regulations that slow AI development.

researchedBills.title: Advancing U.S. Leadership in AI Infrastructure
researchedBills.summary: Opened federal lands for AI data-centre siting and accelerated NEPA environmental review for AI infrastructure.

researchedBills.title: Stargate Project (private-sector endorsement)
researchedBills.summary: Publicly endorsed the $500B OpenAI/SoftBank/Oracle commitment to US AI data-centre buildout.

researchedBills.title: Expanded AI chip export controls on China
researchedBills.summary: Commerce Dept. rule tightening export licensing for advanced AI chips and HBM memory to PRC-aligned buyers.

politicians.name: Xi Jinping
politicians.role: General Secretary, Chinese Communist Party · President of China
politicians.summary: Xi has positioned AI as the centerpiece of China's strategic competition with the US, directing trillions in state and provincial investment into model training, chip self-sufficiency, and data-centre buildout. His administration's 2023 Generative AI Measures and 2024 AI Safety Governance Framework set the global benchmark for state-controlled AI deployment, conditioning model approval on alignment with 'core socialist values'.
keyPoints: Generative AI Measures (Aug. 2023): China's first binding generative-AI rules, requiring CAC pre-approval and content alignment with state values.
AI Safety Governance Framework (Sept. 2024): Established a national risk taxonomy and inter-ministerial review for frontier models.
Approved Made in China 2025 follow-on chip-fab subsidies and Eastern Data, Western Computing data-centre relocation programme.
Frames AI primacy as a CCP-era national objective; routinely invoked in Politburo study sessions and PLA modernisation plans.

researchedBills.title: Interim Measures for the Management of Generative AI Services
researchedBills.summary: First binding national rules on generative AI — requires CAC pre-approval and content alignment with 'core socialist values'.

researchedBills.title: AI Safety Governance Framework (v1.0)
researchedBills.summary: Established a national risk taxonomy and inter-ministerial review process for frontier model deployment.

researchedBills.title: Eastern Data, Western Computing national programme
researchedBills.summary: State-directed relocation of data-centre buildout to Western China, tying approval to renewable-power sites.

researchedBills.title: Made in China 2025 follow-on chip-fab subsidies
researchedBills.summary: Expanded state subsidies for domestic AI chip fabrication as a counter to US export controls.
